lines.en_lit: because the removal of the ashes and the priestly vestments, two verses that come as one, And Any two verses that come as one do not teach,
This well to the Rabbis, who say and shall leave them there” teaches that they require interment.
However, to Rabbi Dosa, who said priestly vestments are fit for a common priest what is there to say?
because removal of the ashes and the heifer whose neck is broken are two verses that come as one, and any two verses that come as one do not teach

It works out well according to the one who said do not teach but according to the one who says that two verses that come as one teach what is there to say? Two exclusions are written “And he shall put it” “Whose neck was broken”
And why do I these three verses with regard to blood,
One to exclude notar, And one to exclude from misuse and one to exclude from ritual impurity.
However, no verse is required to exclude from piggul, as we learned any that has permits it, one is liable to piggul. whether for human or whether on the altar. blood itself, permitted

MISHNA: Each action of Yom Kippur stated in order. If performed actions before another, he has done nothing. he performed the blood of the goat before the blood of the bull, he repeat and sprinkle the blood of the goat after the blood of the bull, And if the blood spills before he completed the presentations that inside he bring other blood, and repeat from the beginning inside

And similarly, in the Sanctuary, and likewise on the golden altar. Since they are each atonement in and of themselves, Rabbi Elazar and Rabbi Shimon say: From the place that he interrupted there he resumes

GEMARA: The Sages taught: Each action of Yom Kippur stated in order. If performed actions before another, he has done nothing. Rabbi Yehuda said: When to those actions performed in white garments inside However, those actions performed in white garments outside, if he performed action before another, what he did is done

Rabbi Neḥemya says: In what is this statement said, to actions performed in white garments, whether inside or outside However, to actions performed in golden garments outside what he did is done. Rabbi Yoḥanan said: And both of them, derived from one verse: “And this shall be an everlasting statute to you, once a year”
